text: He said the terms were perfect, and he told me procurement would sign off.

Welcome to the kick-off, everyone. Let me walk you through the timeline and the workstreams. Each one has a clear owner.
Great. So next week, my team will be onboarding the new users, right? Which workstream will you be assigning to me?
Exactly. You will be owning the onboarding workstream. I will be leading the weekly status update, so we always know if we are on track.
Sounds good. What about the data migration? I am worried it could become a bottleneck.
Good point. We will be monitoring it from day one. By Friday, the team will be running the first sprint, so we will catch any problem early.
Perfect. So this time next month, we will be wrapping up the first phase. Let us get started.

Hi Natalie, this is Tom. Quick briefing before our project kick-off tomorrow. Here is the plan. Next week I will be setting up the project board, and my team will be onboarding the new users. By Friday, we will be running the first sprint, so the timeline is tight but realistic. During March, you will be leading the weekly status calls, and I will be tracking every deliverable against the milestones. One thing to watch: the data migration could become a bottleneck, so we will be monitoring it closely from day one. Could you send me the updated scope before the call? Around this time next month, we will be wrapping up the first phase and reviewing progress with the steering group. Thanks, and talk tomorrow.

Hi, this is Natalie, recording a quick note before the project kick-off. I am excited. After we closed the deal last week, Tom said that his team would handle the technical rollout, and I told him that I would own the weekly updates. So here is how I see the next month. Next week I will be onboarding the client stakeholders and confirming the scope. Throughout March, I will be leading the status calls and tracking each deliverable against our milestones. The biggest risk is the data migration, so we will be watching that bottleneck very carefully. The lesson for me is simple. A clear kick-off sets the tone for the whole project. This time next month, we will be reviewing the first phase, and I want every owner to know exactly what they will be working on.

First, you confirm the scope and the main deliverables with the client. Then you assign an owner to each workstream, so everyone knows their part. After that, you agree on the timeline and the key milestones. Next, you hold the kick-off call and explain what each person will be working on. Finally, you start tracking progress and watching for any bottleneck.
